arguments.task: Open the official Firecrawl website and read the strongest official pages needed to determine: one-line product description, API authentication methods documented by first-party docs, pricing or access tier for API use, API surface type (REST/GraphQL/Both/None), breadth of documented API, whether Firecrawl has an official MCP server or MCP documentation, rate limit notes, and any notable signals. Prefer canonical docs pages, pricing pages, auth pages, API reference pages, and official GitHub/docs pages. Avoid relying on search snippets. Return the final answer as structured notes with the exact supporting canonical URLs you actually visited and read.

input.thought: Use the browser to inspect official Firecrawl docs and pricing/auth pages directly.

raw_result.description: Web data extraction platform for crawling sites and turning web pages into structured or LLM-ready content.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official docs indicate API requests are authenticated with an API key sent as a Bearer token in the Authorization header; no additional first-party auth method was verified from the gathered evidence.
raw_result.gating_reason: Official site exposes self-serve signup and docs for generating an API key, indicating developers can start without sales or admin approval.

api_surface.breadth: Public documentation describes multiple HTTP endpoints for crawling, scraping, mapping, searching, and extracting structured data from websites.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Firecrawl documents an official MCP server for using Firecrawl through the Model Context Protocol.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Rate-limit details were not firmly established from the gathered evidence.
raw_result.notable_signals: Developer docs are public, API reference is available, SDK examples are prominent, and Firecrawl advertises MCP support alongside core crawl/scrape/extract features.
raw_result.human_review_reason: This conclusion is based on limited prior evidence gathered before the browser task completed; structured fields such as access_tier and some supporting URLs should be human-checked against the live official pages.

result.description: Web data extraction platform for crawling sites and turning web pages into structured or LLM-ready content.
result.auth_notes: Official docs indicate API requests are authenticated with an API key sent as a Bearer token in the Authorization header; no additional first-party auth method was verified from the gathered evidence.
result.gating_reason: Official site exposes self-serve signup and docs for generating an API key, indicating developers can start without sales or admin approval.

api_surface.breadth: Public documentation describes multiple HTTP endpoints for crawling, scraping, mapping, searching, and extracting structured data from websites.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Firecrawl documents an official MCP server for using Firecrawl through the Model Context Protocol.

result.rate_limit_notes: Rate-limit details were not firmly established from the gathered evidence.
result.notable_signals: Developer docs are public, API reference is available, SDK examples are prominent, and Firecrawl advertises MCP support alongside core crawl/scrape/extract features.
result.human_review_reason: This conclusion is based on limited prior evidence gathered before the browser task completed; structured fields such as access_tier and some supporting URLs should be human-checked against the live official pages.
